evals.prompt: I need to share user authentication state across my entire React app. Which state management should I use?
evals.expected_output: For auth state (low-frequency, global): Context API is appropriate — create AuthContext with user + logout function. For high-frequency global updates, prefer Zustand (no Provider boilerplate, minimal re-renders). For auth specifically, pair with React Query/SWR to handle the session fetch and cache.

evals.prompt: I'm fetching user data in useEffect and storing it in useState. When I mutate it, the UI goes out of sync.
evals.expected_output: Violation: No server cache as UI State. Use React Query or SWR for server data — they maintain cache consistency. Never sync server data into useState manually. useMutation from React Query handles optimistic updates and cache invalidation automatically. Delete the useEffect/useState pattern.

evals.prompt: My Redux store has 50 slices and managing the boilerplate is overwhelming the team.
evals.expected_output: Consider migrating to Zustand for simpler apps or staying with Redux Toolkit (RTK) for large apps. RTK reduces Redux boilerplate significantly — use createSlice, createAsyncThunk, RTK Query. If switching: migrate slice by slice, not all at once. Zustand is better for small-medium apps without time-travel debugging needs.

should_not_trigger: Configure Redis pub/sub for real-time notifications
Set up a Kubernetes ingress controller
